L3: https://pluginhive.slack.com/archives/C02E15X8X0C/p1780069727833149

US Customer installed WSS plugin to use it with UPS (CTP-Popular) for HAZMAT shipping.

1. When testing the plugin, the HAZMAT label (as in attached screenshot) is not available from the plugin.
This is mandatory for the customer to use our plugin.

2. Customer wants to add EX numbers to the products as part of DG details. Currently, there is no option available in the plugin to add EX numbers.
From the details available for the UPS plugin, **HazMat Additional Description** field is used to provide this detail.


Need assistance from the team to check feasibility of these 2 requirements. If enhancement is needed, request to share the ETA as well.

URL - https://ravenrocksprecision.com
Account ID - 833d9856-d538-4505-9687-1d7da6a649f1
comments.html_body:
L3: https://pluginhive.slack.com/archives/C02E15X8X0C/p1780069727833149
 
US Customer installed WSS plugin to use it with UPS (CTP-Popular) for HAZMAT shipping.
 
  1. When testing the plugin, the HAZMAT label (as in attached screenshot) is not available from the plugin.
    This is mandatory for the customer to use our plugin.

comments.body:
Hello Kelly,

You have this set up correctly. Disabling Realtime Rates is the right approach for your store's pricing model.

Here is how the two sides of the plugin work together:

1. Checkout rates (what customers see): These are controlled by your existing WooCommerce shipping rules — your flat rate of $12.95 for orders under $150 and free shipping for orders over $150. With Realtime Rates disabled, WSS will not push live UPS rates to the checkout, so your existing rules stay in charge. This is exactly what you want.

2. Label generation: WSS still uses your connected UPS account to generate labels on the back end. Your negotiated UPS rates apply at the label stage — the checkout display and the label billing are independent of each other.

Looking at your current Store Setup screen, I can also see a couple of things worth noting:

- Fallback Rate is already set to 12.95 — this is correct. If the Realtime Rates feature were ever turned on and UPS returned no rates for a particular shipment, this value would serve as the backup checkout rate, matching your flat rate.
- Debug Mode and Silent Debug Mode are both enabled. These should be turned off once you are satisfied with the setup, as running in debug mode long-term can affect performance. You can uncheck both under Settings > Store Setup and save changes.

In summary: your current configuration — Realtime Rates off, flat rate rules in WooCommerce, WSS used for label generation — is the correct setup for your use case. No further changes are needed on the rates side.

If you run into anything else or want us to walk through any part of the label workflow, please let us know.

Thanks and regards,
Touseef
Team PluginHive

US Customer testing WSS with UPS for Hazmat shipping. They are on CTP-Popular plan.
Customer has a requirement to add **EX number** on the Hazmat label generated from the plugin. Customer has shared a reference DG label from their end where the detail is added. Attached the PDF for reference.
For team reference -
_**EX number** (Explosive Classification) is mandatory when shipping primers with UPS. Primers are classified as **Class 1.4 Explosives** and must be declared and shipped as hazardous materials._

In our UPS plugin for WooCommerce, there is a **HazMat Additional Description** field available in which this detail can be added. Added a screenshot for reference.

Please check and let us know if this field can be made available in WSS as well for UPS.
OR, if there is an alternative field in which EX number can be added, please let us know.

Hello Kelly,

Thank you for your reply.

The error message **"Technical Name must not match the Proper Shipping Name"** is coming directly from UPS when the Technical Name and Hazmat Proper Shipping Name are the same.

The best approach would be to update the Technical Name with at least one change, similar to what you did for **order 91917**.

If you come across this case for any other order, please share the rate or label failure JSON files with the UPS team and review how this can be handled. Alternatively, let us know when you encounter this again, and I will share the rate or label failure logs with Alyssa Raymond to check if there is a way we can handle this better.

Please let us know how it goes or if you need any assistance from our end.

Thanks and Regards,
